commands.repair_reason: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because the PR already contains a focused implementation with sufficient contributor validation and no actionable review findings.; Cleared: The diff adds no dependency, download, secret, permission, or release-path change, and the generated Windows .cmd wrappers are limited to temporary self-test fake engines. (sha=7e6257854957da305a5ed25f42633af57748cff9)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds Windows portability handling for agent-skills validation, including LF checkout for the shell harness, symlink/test capability skips, deterministic subprocess decoding, inaccessible PATH entry handling, and Windows-runnable fake engine wrappers.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Source inspection matches the reported Windows failures, and the PR body includes detailed Windows after-fix validation, but I did not reproduce the failures on a native Windows checkout during this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because the PR already contains a focused implementation with sufficient contributor validation and no actionable review findings.

target.title: fix: harden agent-skills validation on Windows
target.body:
Fixes #36.

## Summary

This PR makes the shared ^Ggent-skills validation path work cleanly from a native Windows contributor setup while preserving the existing Unix/Linux behavior.

It fixes a small cluster of Windows portability failures:

- skip inaccessible PATH entries inside the existing guarded path-resolution block instead of letting Path.exists() raise before the guard;
- decode text-mode subprocess output as UTF-8 with replacement, so reviewer/tool output with invalid bytes is preserved instead of crashing the helper;
- make --self-test-engine-isolation fake engines runnable on Windows by returning .cmd wrappers for generated fake engine scripts;
- skip hardening assertions that require POSIX-only newline filenames or unavailable Windows symlink privilege;
- skip the Ruby install-skills symlink replacement test when the current Windows user cannot create symlinks;
- force LF checkout for the 	est-review-harness shell wrapper so  ash -n works under Git for Windows with core.autocrlf=true.

## Why

On Windows, contributors can fail before they reach the behavior under review:

- inaccessible path entries can raise PermissionError during engine PATH filtering;
- Windows default text decoding can raise UnicodeDecodeError for arbitrary subprocess output;
- extensionless shebang scripts are not executable through Windows CreateProcess;
- newline path components and symlink creation are not reliable Windows test capabilities;
- CRLF shell wrappers can fail  ash -n even though CI checks the same wrapper on Linux.

The fix keeps unsupported platform capabilities explicit through skips and keeps actual helper behavior deterministic across platforms.

## Changed files

- .gitattributes
- scripts/install-skills.test.rb
- skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview
- skills/autoreview/tests/test_autoreview_hardening.py

## Validation

Local validation on Windows 11 Release Preview / 25H2 build 26200.8728, PowerShell 7.6.2, Python 3.12.10, RubyInstaller with DevKit 3.3.11, Node 24.16.0, Git for Windows core.autocrlf=true:

- uby scripts/validate-skills: passed, alidated 5 skills
- uby -c scripts/install-skills: passed, Syntax OK
- uby -c scripts/validate-skills: passed, Syntax OK
- uby scripts/install-skills.test.rb: passed, 2 runs, 6 assertions,  failures,  errors, 1 skips
-  ash -n skills/autoreview/scripts/test-review-harness: passed
- python -m py_compile skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview skills/autoreview/scripts/test-review-harness.py skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview_test.py: passed
- python skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --self-test-config-defaults: passed
- python skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --self-test-fallback-scope: passed
- python skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --self-test-engine-isolation: passed
- python skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --self-test-json-array-parser: passed
- python skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --self-test-opencode-jsonl-parser: passed
- python skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --self-test-opencode-isolation: passed
- python skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --self-test-cursor-jsonl-parser: passed
- python -m unittest skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview_test.py: passed, 11 tests
- python -m unittest skills/autoreview/tests/test_autoreview_hardening.py: passed, 14 tests, 2 skips
- 
ode --check skills/agent-transcript/scripts/agent-transcript: passed
- 
ode --test skills/agent-transcript/scripts/agent-transcript.test.mjs skills/session-viewer/scripts/session-viewer.test.ts: passed, 32 tests
- git diff --check: passed
- python skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --engine codex --thinking high --no-web-search --parallel-tests "python -m unittest skills/autoreview/tests/test_autoreview_hardening.py" --parallel-tests-shell pwsh: clean, no accepted/actionable findings; overall: patch is correct (0.86)

## Notes

- The Ruby symlink test skip is intentional: it only skips when the current user cannot create symlinks, and the existing test still runs where symlink creation is available.
- The hardening test skips are similarly platform-capability skips, not behavior relaxations.
- This PR does not add a Windows CI job; it only removes known blockers for running the existing validation locally on Windows.
target.author: theangrypit

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
